title: Does the abelian group C_4 x C_112 admit a (448,150,50)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: Lean would only formalize a conditional quotient slice. It would not resolve the load-bearing 5-multiplier gap or settle the exact C_4 x C_112 existence question, so formalization is optional archival work rather than a publication seal.
micro_paper_assessment: Fails the bounded micro-paper audit. The surviving claim is stronger than an example, but only as a conditional quotient slice resting on the unresolved 5-multiplier lane; the remaining gap is not genuinely small.
family_affinity: high; residual-case multiplier lane for order-448 abelian difference sets with exact 2-primary sensitivity
generalization_signal: moderate; the multiplier-plus-small-quotient template may extend to nearby C_(2^a) x C_(2^b) x C_p cases, but the present quotient did not yet eliminate the final lift
strongest_honest_claim: Assuming a valid 5-multiplier normalization for this exact row, any (448,150,50)-difference set in C_4 x C_112 has C_7-fiber sizes (30,20,20,20,20,20,20) and, after quotienting the 2-part by C_4 x 4C_16, a unique coarse residue profile up to inversion: heavy fiber (2,12,8,8), nonzero fiber (6,4,4,6).
paper_title_hint: No current publication title is recommended; at most: 'Conditional Fiber and Quotient-Residue Constraints for a Hypothetical (448,150,50) Difference Set in C_4 x C_112.'
candidate_theorem_slice: Assuming a valid 5-multiplier normalization, the exact row [4,112] reduces to one forced C_7-fiber profile and one coarse C_4-quotient residue profile up to inversion.
next_action: Preserve the conditional quotient slice, but move the candidate aside unless a same-lane argument either proves the 5-multiplier step from cited theorems or replaces it with an unconditional obstruction or construction.
